response.justification: While the candidate brings 20 years of solid software engineering across diverse domains and is currently contributing to AI infrastructure at re:cinq, the role requires recognized expertise in AI strategy and demonstrated advisory experience at director level. The AI work is extremely recent (started April 2025, approximately 2 months), lacks evidence of building AI products or platforms, and shows no track record of strategic partnerships with customers or thought leadership on AI transformation. The resume demonstrates strong full-stack technical capabilities and entrepreneurial spirit through GISCollective, but falls short of the specialized AI expertise and strategic positioning essential for a director-level AI strategy role.
strengths: 20 years of diverse software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and current AI infrastructure work
Currently hands-on with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, showing active engagement with cutting-edge AI tooling
Strong full-stack technical foundation with cloud infrastructure knowledge (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) and proven ability to architect complex systems
concerns: AI infrastructure role is extremely recent (April 2025, ~2 months) with no demonstrated track record of recognized AI expertise, products, or platforms
No evidence of director-level strategic advisory experience—resume shows strong individual contributor roles but no customer advisory, C-level partnerships, or strategic business decision-making
Bachelor's degree in CS only (no Master's or PhD in AI/ML/related field) limits the specialized AI research and innovation depth typically expected for this strategic role
